title: cheilanthes semiglabra
intro: Cheilanthes semiglabra, also known as the half-glabrous lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae, characterized by its distinctive fronds with wavy or lobed margins. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in China and other parts of Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cheilanthes semiglabra include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and digestive disorders. Cheilanthes semiglabra is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its dried fronds can be used in teas, tinctures, or capsules, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
